record_request_information.record_request_information:
I am submitting this Open Public Records Act (OPRA) request as a private resident of the Borough of Prospect Park for the purpose of better understanding recent municipal budgeting and local development decisions in my neighborhood. I live at 142 North 10th Street, and over the last two years I have noticed a combination of increased truck traffic, several new multi-family homes under construction within a few blocks, and rising property taxes on my home. Rather than rely on social media posts and rumors, I want to review the underlying public records myself so that I can participate in Borough Council meetings in a more informed way.

To that end, I am requesting the following records, limited to the time period from January 1, 2023 through November 30, 2025, unless otherwise noted. If any of these records are readily available in electronic format (PDF, Word, Excel, or scanned images), I prefer to receive them electronically by email in order to reduce copying costs.

1. ANNUAL MUNICIPAL BUDGETS AND SUPPORTING DOCUMENTS
   a. A copy of the adopted Borough of Prospect Park municipal budgets for calendar years 2023, 2024, and any adopted 2025 budget or most recent introduced budget, including:
      - The full budget document as adopted by the Mayor and Council.
      - Any budget message, explanatory statement, or narrative that accompanied the adopted budget.
      - Any publicly available schedules or appendices that break down line-item appropriations and revenues (e.g., police, public works, planning/zoning, legal, engineering, debt service, and capital improvements).
   b. Any publicly available summary sheets or presentations prepared for the public, the Mayor, or the Borough Council that explain major changes between the 2022 and 2023 budgets, and between the 2023 and 2024 budgets, particularly items related to:
      - Capital improvements in the vicinity of North 8th Street, North 9th Street, and North 10th Street.
      - Road resurfacing, stormwater/drainage work, or sidewalk improvements in the above area.

2. PROPERTY TAX AND ASSESSMENT INFORMATION FOR MY PROPERTY
   a. For the property located at 142 North 10th Street, Prospect Park, NJ 07508 (Block and Lot number if available in your system—if you can look it up using the street address, that is fine):
      - The current property tax assessment card or record showing land value, improvement value, and total assessed value for tax years 2022, 2023, 2024, and 2025 (if available).
      - Any notice of assessment change, revaluation, or reassessment issued to this property for those years, including the reason for the change if it is documented.
      - A breakdown of the total property tax bill for 2023 and 2024, including the municipal, county, and school portions, and any special assessments if applicable. I am not asking for my payment history, only the official bill or billing summary showing how the total was calculated.

3. ZONING, LAND USE, AND DEVELOPMENT APPROVALS NEAR MY RESIDENCE
   I am particularly interested in the cluster of recent and ongoing construction projects within approximately a 3–4 block radius of my home. To narrow the request, please focus on:
   - North 8th Street, North 9th Street, and North 10th Street between Haledon Avenue and North 4th Street; and
   - Any properties within 500 feet of the intersection of North 10th Street and Haledon Avenue.

   For the above-described area, please provide for the period January 1, 2023 through November 30, 2025:
   a. A list (or individual records if a list is not available) of all applications submitted to the Planning Board or Zoning Board of Adjustment involving:
      - New multi-family residential construction (three or more dwelling units).
      - Major site plan approvals.
      - Use variances or density variances.
   b. For each such application, please provide:
      - The application form submitted by the applicant (with any confidential personal identifiers redacted as required by law).
      - The resolution of memorialization adopted by the Planning Board or Zoning Board, including conditions of approval.
      - Any staff reports from the Borough Planner, Borough Engineer, or other professional consultants that were prepared for the Board and are part of the public file.
      - Any publicly available traffic or parking studies submitted in connection with the application.

4. TRUCK TRAFFIC AND ROADWAY SAFETY STUDIES
   a. Any traffic studies, engineering reports, or consultant memoranda prepared for or obtained by the Borough between January 1, 2023 and November 30, 2025 that analyze:
      - Truck traffic volumes, cut-through traffic, or commercial vehicle routing on North 10th Street or adjacent streets.
      - Pedestrian safety, crosswalks, or speeding concerns in the immediate area of North 10th Street between Haledon Avenue and North 4th Street.
   b. Any correspondence (letters or emails) between the Borough and Passaic County or the State of New Jersey Department of Transportation that specifically references truck routing, traffic calming, or roadway safety improvements affecting North 10th Street or Haledon Avenue in Prospect Park during the same time period.

5. COUNCIL MEETING MINUTES AND RECORDINGS
   a. Copies of the approved minutes of the Borough Council meetings from January 1, 2023 through November 30, 2025 in which any of the following topics were discussed:
      - Revaluation or reassessment of residential properties.
      - Adoption of the annual municipal budget.
      - Major development projects, rezonings, or redevelopment areas in the vicinity of North 8th, 9th, or 10th Streets.
      - Traffic calming, truck restrictions, or pedestrian safety measures near Haledon Avenue.
   b. If available and reasonably retrievable without an undue burden, links to or copies of any audio or video recordings of those meetings (for example, if they are already posted on the Borough website or YouTube channel, a list of URLs would be sufficient).

FORMAT AND DELIVERY PREFERENCES
- I prefer that all responsive records be provided in electronic format via email whenever possible. PDF format is ideal for scanned documents or formal resolutions/minutes, and Excel/CSV is preferred for any tabular data such as tax or assessment tables.
- If certain records are only available in paper form and would require significant scanning or copying that triggers additional costs under OPRA, please notify me in advance if the total cost is expected to exceed the maximum authorization amount I have indicated below so that I can decide whether to narrow or modify my request.

I understand that some documents may contain personal identifiers or information that must be redacted under OPRA or other applicable privacy laws (for example, Social Security Numbers, specific bank account information, or certain personal contact details of private individuals). I am not seeking any such confidential information and have no objection to appropriate redactions. If parts of my request are unclear or overly broad, I would appreciate the Records Custodian contacting me so that we can clarify or narrow the request, rather than denying it outright. I am making this request solely as a resident who wants to be more engaged in local government and not on behalf of any commercial entity or law firm.
